author: maintainer-agent

patch.value: Scorecard Branch-Protection (-1→10): give the Scorecard workflow read access to protection rules

Scorecard Branch-Protection returns a real score instead of -1: either main's protections are expressed as repository RULESETS (readable by the default token) or a fine-grained PAT with administration:read is stored as a repo secret and wired into scorecard.yml per the scorecard-action fine-grained-auth docs; chosen approach + rationale recorded here

message: Scorecard errors: default GITHUB_TOKEN cannot read CLASSIC branch protection. The repo already emits 'Bypassed rule violations' on pushes, so rulesets are at least partially in use — auditing whether classic protection can be fully migrated to rulesets is the zero-secret path; else HUMAN-GATED secret creation (maintainer must mint the PAT). Free features only.

author: codex-audit-20260715

patch.value: Give Scorecard reliable read access to branch-protection policy through repository rulesets or a least-privilege credential, with the chosen approach documented.
